content: The Finnish Ministry of Defence reported that several low-flying, slow-moving small objects were detected in Finnish airspace on Sunday morning over sea areas and Southeast Finland. The Air Force deployed an F/A-18 Hornet fighter for identification. Two drones were confirmed to have crashed—one north of Kouvola and another east of Kouvola. Police have secured the crash sites for further investigation. Defense Minister Antti Häkkänen emphasized the seriousness of the incident, stating that drones had entered Finnish territory and that security authorities responded immediately. The Defence Forces are investigating the matter in cooperation with other authorities.
